item.title: Django 可用于您的下一个项目的 5 大理由 | Top 5 Reasons why Django can be used for your next project
item.description:
在开始一个新项目时，在选择语言和框架时做出决定是非常关键的。
大多数时候，我们很想根据别人的想法和我们以前在项目或产品上的经验来做出决定，尽管这可能不会对我们有利。 但是，最好对重要的事实和因素进行评估并指向该特定主题。 尽管如此，了解可扩展性、安全性、开发过程并最终设定交付成功项目的目标也很重要。

如果您正在计划开发 Web 或移动应用程序的下一个项目并且不确定框架。 我建议你应该去 Django。 由于它是一个非常强大的基于 Python 的框架，因此大多数开发人员在开发此类项目时都会选择它。 不过，您可能有很多选择框架的理由； 这里有一些让您的工作更轻松的理由。

选择 Django 的主要原因

在科技市场上，企业在做项目时会有很多选择，但选择最好的是非常关键的。

优秀社区

我们都知道 Python 是最受欢迎的编程语言，而 Django 是它的社区之一； 它拥有大量的支持者和用户。 因此，大多数开源项目都交付给了这个社区。 这表明如果你遇到麻烦，你会找一个助手来解决你的问题。

提供高安全性

在此框架中，默认情况下会打开安全功能。 例如，它可以防止跨站点编程、请求伪造、点击劫持和 SQL 注入。 高度可扩展

创建在线应用程序时，扩展能力至关重要。 为了最大限度地提高可扩展性和降低网络托管成本，该框架还可以同时管理数百万用户的流量。 此外，Django 允许使用插件来扩展您的 Web 应用程序，并且可以随时使用大量的包来帮助您扩展产品。

建立在DRY原则之上

不要重复自己，或 DRY，允许在专注于原始代码的同时重用以前编写的代码。 可移植性

因此，无论是开发环境还是生产环境都不受支持。 如果这还不够，Django 还带有一个 ORM（对象关系映射器），一个位于程序员和数据库之间的层，使您只需更改一行代码即可在数据库之间切换项目。

相关文章： Flash 与 Django

使用 Django 的顶级公司。

  • 平特雷斯特
  • 国家地理
  • 摩斯拉
  • 普雷齐
  • 讨论区
  • 活动现场
  • 比特桶

结语

正如我们所见，Django 是一个用于创建 Web 应用程序的出色框架，因为它提供了大量显着优势并可以访问无穷无尽的资源。 希望您能够对有关 Django 在开发 Web 或移动应用程序方面的效率如何的文章感兴趣。 除了制作出色应用程序的这些原因之外，您还需要聘请 Django 开发人员，他们将帮助您成功实现您的梦想项目。



原文:

It is very crucial to make decisions while choosing a language and framework when starting a new project.
Most of the time, we are tempted to make decisions based on other people's thoughts and on the previous experiences that we have worked on projects or products, although it might not turn to our favorably. However, it's better to make an evaluation of the facts and factors that are important and point to this particular topic. With all this, it is also essential to know the scalability, security, development process, and eventually set a goal to deliver a successful project.

If you are planning your next project for developing web or mobile applications and are unsure of the framework. I suggest you should go for Django. As it is a very powerful Python-based framework, most developers choose it while developing such types of projects. Still, you might have many reasons for the selection of the framework; here are some reasons to make your work easier.

Top Reasons why to choose Django

In the tech market, companies will have lot many options while making the projects, but choosing the best is very crucial.

Excellent community

We all know Python is the most preferred programming language, and Django is among its community; it has a huge number of supporters and users. As a result, most of the open-source projects are delivered to this community. This shows that if you are running into trouble, you will find an assistant to solve your problems.

Providing high-Security

Django's ability to build applications more quickly and deliver them without sacrificing their security is one of the most powerful features of the rest. In this framework, security features are turned on by default. It has safeguards against cross-site programming, request forgery, clickjacking, and SQL injection, for instance. Django regularly provides new security updates, and it notifies other frameworks of security flaws and fixes them right away.

Highly scalable

The ability to scale is crucial when creating online applications. Scalability is made simple by Django's default inclusion of several pluggable and replaceable components enabling more specialized solutions. To maximize scalability and lower web hosting costs, the framework can also manage the traffic of millions of users at a time. Additionally, Django enables the usage of plugins to extend your web application, and an overload of packages is readily available to assist you in scaling up your product.

Established on the DRY principle

Don't Repeat Yourself, or DRY, allows for the reuse of previously written code while concentrating on the the original. Django actually makes it possible for quick development. The Django ware Foundation asserts that Django handles a significant portion of the pain associated with the actual development process, allowing developers to concentrate on their applications and deliver them as quickly as possible without sacrificing security.

Portability

Django is compatible with almost every platform, including Mac, PC, and Linux, much like Python is. So, neither a development environment nor a production environment is unsupported. If that still wasn't enough, Django also comes with an ORM (object-relational mapper), a layer that sits between the programmer and the database and enables you to switch projects among databases by just altering a single line of code.

Related article: Flash vs Django

Top Companies that are using Django.

  • Spotify
  • Pinterest
  • National Geographic
  • Instagram
  • Mozilla
  • Prezi
  • Disqus
  • Eventbrite
  • Bitbucket

Closing thoughts

As we've seen, Django is a fantastic framework for creating web applications since it offers a tonne of significant advantages and access to an endless number of resources. Hope you were able to least your interest in the article on how effective Django can be in developing web or mobile applications. In addition to these reasons to make outstanding applications, you need to hire Django developers who will be helping you to make your dream project successful.

item.description:
保罗·勒费弗尔

发表于 1 月 11 日

大约一年前，我开始了一个我称之为Goto 10的逆向计算时事通讯/博客。

这当然是对我第一次学习如何编码时使用的老式 BASIC 编程语言的一种演绎。 规范程序是：

Paul Lefebvre

Posted on Jan 11

About a year ago I started a retrocomputing newsletter/blog that I call Goto 10.

That is of course a play on the old-school BASIC programming languages that I used when first learning how to code. The canonical program is:

10 PRINT "HELLO"
20 GOTO 10

Not all the posts there are about software development or programming, but several of them have been about using BASIC or Pascal.

If you're interested in this sort of thing, I encourage you to check it out at:

www.goto10retro.com

item.title: 如何在前端项目上设置 Firebase | How setup Firebase on your Frontend project
item.description:
尼古拉斯·蒙铁尔

发表于 1 月 12 日

嘿！ 在这里，我将帮助您在前端项目上进行 Firebase 的初始配置。 如果您使用 vanilla Javascript 或 Vue 或 React 等框架，这也会很有用。

配置 Firebase 帐户

首先，我们需要在 Firebase 上创建一个帐户并添加一个新项目。

之后，您将可以访问项目的控制台。 在项目设置中，您可以获得我们要安装的 SDK 的凭据。

让我们写一些代码吧！

首先，您需要安装 SDK。

之后，我建议创建一个名为firebase.ts或类似文件的文件，您可以在其中初始化 Firebase 并在需要时导入，而不是全局使用它。

另外，请避免将您的凭据直接存储在您的代码中，有像dotenv这样的包来处理这种类型的东西。

这很简单，但让我们解释一些行：

  • 第 5 行：在这里我们创建我们的配置对象。 这与您在 Firebase 控制台上看到的完全一样。
  • 第 6 行：如果你从未见过import.meta.env.KEY_NAME ，它只是Vite 用于环境变量的命名约定。 所以，如果你不使用 Vite，不用担心这个。
  • 第 17、19 行：初始化并导出您的 Firebase 对象。

是的，就像那样。

使用此 Firebase 对象，您可以开始使用 Firebase 提供的不同服务（如数据库、身份验证等）。
这是他们自己的文档中的示例。 当您要初始化其他服务时，此 Firebase 对象始终是一个参数。

原文:

Nicolás Montiel

Posted on Jan 12

Hey! :D

Here, I will help you to make the initial configuration of Firebase on your Frontend proyect. This also will be useful if you use vanilla Javascript or a framework like Vue or React.

Configure the Firebase Account

First, we need to create an account on Firebase and add a New Project.

After that, you will have access to the Console of your project. On the Project Settings you can get your credentials for the SDK that we going to install.

Let's write some code!

First, you need to install the SDK.

npm i firebase or yarn add firebase

After, I recommend to create a file called firebase.ts or something like that, where you can initialize Firebase and just imported when is needed, instead of using it globally.

Also, please avoid store your credentials directly on your code, there are packages like dotenv to this type of stuffs.

It's pretty simple, but let's explain some lines:

  • Line 5: Here we create our configuration object. This is exactly the same that you saw on your Firebase Console.
  • Line 6: If you never saw the import.meta.env.KEY_NAME, it's just the name convention that Vite uses for the enviroment variables. So, if you don't use Vite, don't worry about this.
  • Line 17, 19: Initialize and export your Firebase object.

Yeah, just like that.

With this Firebase object, you can start using the different services that Firebase offers (Like databases, authentication, etc).
Here is an example in their own documentation. This Firebase Object always is a parameter when you want to initialize other service.

☝️ Check out this all-time classic DEV post

item.title: 建立可靠的 CI 系统所需的 6 个主要技巧 | 6 master tips you'll ever need to set up reliable CI systems
item.description:
帕拉扬特里帕蒂

发表于 1 月 12 日

设置持续集成是每个 DevOps 工程师被要求做的最多余的工作之一。

以下是您设置持续集成 (CI) 系统的 6 条规则：

随着系统的增长，依赖关系也随之悄然增长。 它变得脆弱，容易折断，而且很难安装。
不要让任何你不能用脚本设置的东西进入你的构建服务器

尽你所能使依赖服务的设置和拆除保持一致，并知道它们何时完成。

定期重构测试，甚至重构整个构建过程，将会提高开发人员的整体生产力。

鼓励模拟和假人，并在测试中设置模式。
您对外部资源进行测试的依赖越少，测试运行验证核心逻辑的速度就越快。

如果您有完全自动化的验收测试和用户场景，您的整个产品可以更加敏捷和有效地移动，随着业务需求而发展。

内存占用、CPU 和 IO 预算、调用跟踪以及用户交互和计算密集型功能的计时。 这使您能够发现复杂系统中的意外故障

我希望这些规则可以帮助您为您的公司建立一个有效的 CI 系统。

感谢您阅读本文。

如果您喜欢此内容，可以关注我或在 pragyanatvade 的 Twitter 上获取更多此类精彩内容！

弗兰克罗斯纳 - 22 年 11 月 11 日

一旦暂停，pragyanatvade 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，pragyanatvade 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，pragyanatvade 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 pragyanatvade 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 pragyanatvade：

降低 pragyanatvade 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 pragyanatvade 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Pragyan Tripathi

Posted on Jan 12

Setting up continuous integration is one of the most redundant works every DevOps engineer is asked to do.

Here are 6 rules for you to set up Continuous Integration (CI) Systems:

1. Keep all the logic for building your code in scripts in your git repository.

As the system grows, dependencies grow with it silently. It becomes fragile, easy to break, and hard to set up.
Don't let anything into your build server that you can’t set up with a script

2. Gate merging code on the results of tests, and mercilessly hunt down and resolve unstable tests.

Do everything you can to make the setup and tear down of your dependent services consistent and know when they are complete.

3. Speed of your gates will directly impact developer velocity and productivity, keep it lean and be consistent with mocks or dummies in your testing code.

Periodic refactoring of the tests, or even the whole build process, will pay off in overall developer productivity.

4. Make sure all the developers can reproduce what gets run in the CI system by themselves.

Encourage mocks and dummies, and setup up patterns in your tests.
The less you depend on external resources for tests, the faster your test run will be to validate core logic.

5. Cascade your builds and tests, driving full integration tests with CI systems.

If you have acceptance tests and user scenarios fully automated, your whole product can move far more agilely and effectively, evolving with the business needs.

6. Develop, measure, and review benchmarks about your product from your CI systems.

Memory footprints, CPU and IO budgets, tracing of calls and timing for user interactions and computationally intensive functions. This enables you to find unexpected failures in a complex system

I hope these rules help you set up an effective CI system for your company.

Thanks for reading this.

If you liked this content you can follow me or on Twitter at pragyanatvade for more such awesome content!

Pavan Belagatti - Dec 9 '22

Nelson Hernández - Dec 11 '22

Frank Rosner - Nov 11 '22

Leober Ramos - Nov 29 '22

item.title: 7 个鲜为人知的 CSS 技巧来编写更好的流线型 CSS 代码 | 7 lesser-Known CSS Tricks to write better Streamlined CSS Code
item.description:
它可以为您的网站、登陆页面增添许多美感，并可以让您的项目脱颖而出。 但是很难理解 CSS，因为没有调试工具来查找 CSS 中的缺陷和错误。 但是通过不断的练习和更广为人知的技能，您可以编写漂亮且流线型的 CSS 代码，从而增强网站的整体外观。

无论是您的作品集项目、Professional 项目还是任何其他项目，出色的 CSS 技巧都可以使您的网站看起来异常出色和有趣。

以下是您必须遵循的一些鲜为人知的 CSS 技巧：

它可以用作其他几个属性的简写，也可以用来为所有元素重置一个属性。

这对于选择除一个元素以外的所有元素或仅选择组中的某些元素很有用。

它可用于选择每隔一个元素，或根据更复杂的模式选择元素。

这些伪类允许您选择父元素中特定类型的第一个或最后一个元素。 例如，您可以使用“:first-of-type”来选择“div”中的第一个“p”元素。

这是一个伪类，当它处于占位符显示状态时选择输入元素。

这些属性允许您控制背景图像在元素中的绘制位置。 这是一个包装。 谢谢阅读。

关注我，了解有关技术领域的每周新花絮。

需要顶级 UI/UX 设计师和前端开发自由职业者来解决您的开发难题？ 在Upwork和Freelancer上联系我

想看看我在做什么？ 查看我的Twitter和GitHub 。

想要为您的网站和社交媒体发展提供更好的服务？ 看看我的代理商Auxo digitals。

想要连接？ 在LinkedIn上联系我。

我是一名自由职业者，将于 2022 年年中开始成为数字游民。 想赶上旅程？ 在Instagram 上关注我。



原文:

CSS or Cascading Style Sheets can make your websites came out alive. It can add so much beauty to your websites, landing pages, and can make your projects stand out from the rest of the crowd. But it is very difficult to understand CSS as there's no debugging tool to find bugs and errors in CSS. But with constant practice and with better known skills you can write beautiful and streamlined CSS code that can enhance the overall look of your website.

Whether it's your portfolio project, Professional, or any other projects, exceptional CSS tricks can make your website exceptionally great and fun to look.

Here are some of the lesser-known CSS tricks you must follow:

The "all" property

The "all" property allows you to apply a CSS property to all elements on a page. It can be used as a shorthand for several other properties, and it can also be used to reset a property for all elements.

The ":not" pseudo-class

The ":not" pseudo-class allows you to select elements that do not match a certain criteria. This can be useful for selecting all elements except one, or for selecting only certain elements within a group.

The ":nth-child" pseudo-class

The ":nth-child" pseudo-class allows you to select a specific child element based on its position within a parent element. It can be used to select every other element, or to select elements based on more complex patterns.

The ":first-of-type" and ":last-of-type" pseudo-classes

These pseudo-classes allow you to select the first or last element of a specific type within a parent element. For example, you can use ":first-of-type" to select the first "p" element within a "div".

The ":only-of-type" pseudo-class

The ":only-of-type" pseudo-class allows you to select an element if it is the only one of its type within its parent.

The ":placeholder-shown" pseudo-class

This is a pseudo-class that selects the input element when it is in a placeholder-showing state.

The "background-clip" and "background-origin" properties

These properties allow you to control where the background image is drawn within an element. The "background-clip" property allows you to control how far the background extends into the element's padding, and the "background-origin" property allows you to control where the background image is positioned within the element.

That’s a wrap. Thanks for reading.

Follow me for weekly new tidbits on the domain of tech.

Need a Top Rated UI/UX designer and Front-End Development Freelancer to chop away your development woes? Contact me on Upwork and Freelancer

Want to see what I am working on? Check out my Twitter, and GitHub.

Want better services for your website and social media growth? Look out my agency Auxo digitals.

Want to connect? Reach out to me on LinkedIn.

I am a freelancer who will start off as a Digital Nomad in mid-2022. Want to catch the journey? Follow me on Instagram.

item.title: 我们询问了 ChatGPT 如何使用我们的服务。 这是AI的回答 | We asked ChatGPT how to use our service. Here is what AI answered
item.description:
欢迎来到 2023 年！ 在这里，我们有可以轻松取代真人的聊天机器人！

但后来我们想到：“如果我们要求 ChatGPT 使用 Uploadcare 构建一个文件上传器页面会怎么样？” 为什么不？ 应该很有趣。

我们决定扮演初级开发人员的角色，而不是在一开始就向机器人提供上下文。 我们的“开发人员”听说过前端、HTML、CSS 和 JS，但他们了解的并不多。 这就是为什么他们会问很多新手问题。 为了更容易阅读：我们的问题是标题，而 ChatGPT 的答案是普通文本。 斜体文字是我们对整部剧的评论。

我们按原样使用了机器人的答案。 我们唯一删除的是无处不在的提醒，即机器人已准备好回答任何问题。 机器人会在每个回复的末尾说出来。

让我们开始吧？

如何使用文件输入创建一个简单的 HTML 网页？

这是一个简单的 HTML 网页，其中包含一个文件输入字段，允许用户选择要上传的文件：

 <html> <body> <form> <label for="file">Select a file:</label><br> <input type="file" id="file" name="file"><br> <input type="submit" value="Submit"> </form> </body> </html>

此 HTML 代码创建一个带有文件输入字段和提交按钮的表单。 当用户选择文件并单击提交按钮时，将提交表单并上传所选文件。

如何在本地主机上提供一个简单的 HTML 文件以便能够在浏览器中打开它？

要在本地主机上提供 HTML 文件，您需要使用网络服务器。 以下是使用简单的 Python Web 服务器在本地主机上提供 HTML 文件的步骤：

  1. 安装 Python：确保您的计算机上安装了 Python。 您可以通过打开终端并输入命令python --version来检查是否已安装 Python。 如果未安装 Python，您可以从 Python 网站下载并安装它。

  2. 启动 Web 服务器：打开终端并导航到包含 HTML 文件的目录。 然后通过输入以下命令启动 Python Web 服务器：

 python -m http.server 8000

这将在您的本地计算机上启动一个侦听端口 8000 的 Web 服务器。

  1. 在浏览器中查看 HTML 文件：打开 Web 浏览器并输入以下 URL：
 http://localhost:8000/your_html_file.html

将“your_html_file.html”替换为您的 HTML 文件的名称。 这将在您的浏览器中打开 HTML 文件。

那么它现在有效：

请给我在我的网站上安装 Uploadcare 小部件的代码

我们正在努力保持礼貌。 永远不知道 AI 什么时候变成天网！

要在您的网站上安装 Uploadcare 小部件，您需要将以下代码添加到您的 HTML：

 <script src="https://ucarecdn.com/libs/widget/3.x/uploadcare.full.min.js" charset="utf-8"></script>

这将包括您网站中的 Uploadcare 小部件库。

将此脚本添加到 HTML 后，您就可以使用该小部件允许用户从您的网站上传文件。 以下是如何执行此操作的示例：

 <!-- HTML for file input --> <input type="hidden" role="uploadcare-uploader" name="my_file" /> <!-- JavaScript to initialize the widget --> <script> UPLOADCARE_PUBLIC_KEY = 'your_public_key'; var widget = uploadcare.Widget('[role=uploadcare-uploader]'); </script>

在此示例中，具有uploadcare-uploader角色的input元素用作小部件的占位符。 同样，它有效：

我们测试了小部件，它工作正常。 但这是安装我们的上传器的旧方法。 目前我们有一个测试版的文件上传器，它基于Uploadcare Blocks 。 让我们看看机器人知道些什么！

我在他们的网站上看到，现在他们提供一种叫做“块”的东西。 如何使用这些创建文件上传器？

它允许您使用最少的代码轻松地将文件上传和文件处理功能添加到您的网站或应用程序。

要使用 Uploadcare Blocks 创建文件上传表单，您需要执行以下操作：

  1. 注册一个 Uploadcare 帐户：转到Uploadcare 网站并注册一个帐户。 您将需要提供您的电子邮件地址并创建一个密码。

  2. 安装 Uploadcare Blocks 库：要使用 Uploadcare Blocks，您需要在 HTML 代码中包含 Blocks 库。 您可以通过将以下脚本标记添加到您的 HTML 来执行此操作：

 <script src="https://ucarecdn.com/libs/blocks/2.x/uploadcare.min.js" charset="utf-8"></script>
  1. 将文件输入元素添加到您的 HTML：要使用 Uploadcare Blocks 创建文件上传表单，您需要将文件输入元素添加到您的 HTML 代码中。 您可以使用以下 HTML 来执行此操作：
 <lr-file-uploader-regular name="my_file"></lr-file-uploader-regular>

这将创建一个文件输入元素，用于选择和上传文件。

  1. 初始化 Uploadcare 块库：要初始化 libaby，请在使用lr-file-uploader-regular元素时设置 Uploadcare 公钥。 您可以将--cfg-pubkey属性用作 CSS 上下文属性。 例如：
 <style> lr-file-uploader-regular { --cfg-pubkey: "your_public_key"; } </style>

将“your_public_key”替换为您可以在帐户设置中找到的实际 Uploadcare 公钥。 这将为文件上传器设置公钥并允许它连接到 Uploadcare API。

哈！ 这次不行了！

让我们问问机器人为什么！

您提供的应该安装库的链接已损坏

对于断开的链接，我深表歉意。 您应该使用此 URL 将 Blocks 库包含在您的 HTML 代码中。

要在您的 HTML 中包含 Blocks 库，您需要将以下脚本标记添加到您的 HTML 中：

 <script src="https://unpkg.com/@uploadcare/blocks@0.14.3/web/blocks-browser.min.js" charset="utf-8"></script>

这将在您的 HTML 中包含 Blocks 库，允许您使用 Blocks 库来构建文件上传器和文件处理工作流。

好吧，聪明的裤子先生，你好像忘记了什么：

谢谢，现在脚本可以运行了，但看起来样式没有加载。 这是我在控制台中看到的：“CSS 数据错误：--icon-upload”。 如何修复样式？

要修复您在控制台中看到的 CSS 错误，您需要在 HTML 中包含 Uploadcare Blocks 库的 CSS 样式。

您可以通过将以下链接元素添加到 HTML 的头部来完成此操作：

 <link href="https://unpkg.com/@uploadcare/blocks@0.14.3/web/file-uploader-regular.min.css" rel="stylesheet">

这将在您的 HTML 中包含 Blocks 库的 CSS 样式，并且应该修复您在控制台中看到的 CSS 错误。

或者，您可以使用lr-file-uploader-regular元素上的css-src属性来指定 Blocks 库的 CSS 样式的 URL。 例如：

 <lr-file-uploader-regular name="my_file" css-src="https://unpkg.com/@uploadcare/blocks@0.14.3/web/file-uploader-regular.min.css" ></lr-file-uploader-regular>

有用！

我们已经成功地使用机器人建议的代码上传了一个文件：

好吧。 当我将图片上传到 Uploadcare 时，如何将它插入到网页中？ 我也想把它调整到 256×256px

要在网页中显示您上传到 Uploadcare 的图像，您需要使用img元素并将src属性设置为图像在 Uploadcare 服务器上的 URL。

例如：

 <img src="https://ucarecdn.com/image_id/">

将“image_id”替换为您要显示的图像的实际 ID。 您可以在上传图片时 Blocks 库返回的响应中找到图片的 ID。

要将图像调整为特定大小，可以在图像的 URL 中使用-/resize/操作。 例如，要将图像调整为 256×256 像素，您可以使用以下 URL：

 <img src="https://ucarecdn.com/image_id/-/resize/256x256/">

这将显示图像的 256×256 像素版本。 您可以通过更改resize操作中的值来指定所需的任何大小。

十分简单：

现在，让我们总结一下没有斜体的实验。

结论

老实说，当我们开始实验时，我们不相信 ChatGPT 可以处理它。 当然，这是一个很酷的玩具，它甚至知道如何生成与您的问题相关的代码示例。 但令我们惊讶的是，该机器人对我们的新功能、最近的文档等了解很多。

感觉就像您正在与可以为您谷歌搜索的助手交谈。

在第一次狂喜过后，我们向 ChatGPT 询问了更多与编程相关的问题。 我们会说它生成的代码看起来应该可以工作。 如果您不知道如何解决问题，您得到的代码可能会误导您，因为它太有说服力了。

此外，ChatGPT 也会出错。 在本文中，我们只遇到过一个——断开的链接。 但是你问的问题越多，机器人的答案就越有关联。 有时他们变得过于紧密并开始相互影响。 例如，ChatGPT 会根据您喜欢的语言或编程范式调整回复。 此“功能”有时会导致代码损坏或未优化。

不管怎样，ChatGPT 仍然是一个方便使用的工具。 有一些用例：

  1. 收集一些主题的广泛概述。 例如，您可能会询问科学概念或发现。 当机器人回答时，您可能会询问与其他概念的关系、该领域的关键人物等。 这就像在 Google 中阅读那些维基百科片段，但您可以针对您的任何问题获得这样的片段。 尝试： “请告诉我，艾伦图灵最受欢迎的论文是关于什么的？”

  2. 处理您网站或应用程序上的交互式聊天（没办法！）。 或者帮助呼叫中心的人员在一堆文档中找到答案。

  3. 生成无聊的文本块，例如法律文件或公函。 当然，律师应该审查输出，但结果还不错。 尝试： “我正在创建一个不收集任何数据的安卓游戏。它甚至没有连接到互联网。你介意为游戏生成隐私政策吗？”

  4. 从提供的文本中提取 SEO 关键字。 但我们将看看谷歌对此有何看法。

  5. 将复杂事物的描述简化到所需的水平。 例如，尝试从维基百科复制粘贴科学术语定义，并要求 ChatGPT 为二年级学生简化它。

  6. 一起解决难题。 它可以轻松解决填字游戏。 如果你努力尝试，你甚至可以解释 bot Wordle 规则并获得今天单词的帮助！

最后，如果你有一家科技公司，看看机器人是否可以使用你的服务是很有趣的。 这种检查的结果可能会促使您改进文档或阐明产品的用途。

试一试，谁知道呢，您可能会在您的工具集中找到 ChatGPT 的一席之地。

天哪，多么美好的时光啊！

最初由 Igor Adamenko 在Uploadcare 博客中发表



原文:

Welcome to 2023! Here we have chatbots that can easily replace real humans!

OpenAI launched ChatGPT in November 2022. We played with this bot, discovered some appealing applications, and forgot about it.

But then we thought: “What if we ask ChatGPT to build a file uploader page using Uploadcare?” Why not? It should be entertaining.

Instead of providing the context to the bot at the start, we decided to play a role of a junior developer. Our “developer” heard something about frontend, HTML, CSS & JS, but they do not know much. This is why they ask a lot of newbie questions. To make it easier to read: our questions are headlines, and ChatGPT’s answers are the normal text. Italic text is our comments on the whole play.

We used the bot’s answers as is. The only thing we removed is the ubiquitous reminders that the bot is ready for any questions. The bot says it at the end of each reply.

Let’s get started, huh?

How to create a simple HTML webpage with a file input?

Here is a simple HTML webpage with a file input field that allows users to select a file to upload:

<html> <body> <form> <label for="file">Select a file:</label><br> <input type="file" id="file" name="file"><br> <input type="submit" value="Submit"> </form> </body>
</html>

This HTML code creates a form with a file input field and a submit button. When the user selects a file and clicks the submit button, the form will be submitted and the selected file will be uploaded.

How to serve a simple HTML file on localhost to be able to open it in a browser?

To serve an HTML file on localhost, you will need to use a web server. Here are the steps to serve an HTML file on localhost using a simple Python web server:

  1. Install Python: Make sure you have Python installed on your computer. You can check if Python is already installed by opening a terminal and entering the command python --version. If Python is not installed, you can download and install it from the Python website.

  2. Start the web server: Open a terminal and navigate to the directory that contains your HTML file. Then start the Python web server by entering the following command:

 python -m http.server 8000

This will start a web server on your local machine that listens on port 8000.

  1. View the HTML file in your browser: Open a web browser and enter the following URL: 
   http://localhost:8000/your_html_file.html

Replace “your_html_file.html” with the name of your HTML file. This will open the HTML file in your browser.

Well it works for now:

Give me code that installs Uploadcare widget on my website, please

We are trying to be polite. Never know when AI becomes Skynet!

To install the Uploadcare widget on your website, you will need to add the following code to your HTML:

<script src="https://ucarecdn.com/libs/widget/3.x/uploadcare.full.min.js" charset="utf-8"></script>

This will include the Uploadcare widget library in your website.

Once you have added this script to your HTML, you can then use the widget to allow users to upload files from your website. Here is an example of how you can do this:

<!-- HTML for file input -->
<input type="hidden" role="uploadcare-uploader" name="my_file" /> <!-- JavaScript to initialize the widget -->
<script> UPLOADCARE_PUBLIC_KEY = 'your_public_key'; var widget = uploadcare.Widget('[role=uploadcare-uploader]');
</script>

In this example, the input element with the uploadcare-uploader role serves as a placeholder for the widget. The JavaScript code initializes the widget and specifies your Uploadcare public key, which you can find in your account settings.

Again, it works:

We tested the widget, it works properly. But this is an old way to install our uploader. Currently we have a beta version of File Uploader, that is based on Uploadcare Blocks. Let’s find out what the bot knows about it!

I see on their website, that now they offer something called “Blocks”. How do I create a file uploader using these?

Uploadcare Blocks is a new way to build file uploaders and file handling workflows using pre-built UI components. It allows you to easily add file uploading and file handling functionality to your website or application with minimal code.

To create a file uploading form using Uploadcare Blocks, you will need to do the following:

  1. Sign up for an Uploadcare account: Go to the Uploadcare website and sign up for an account. You will need to provide your email address and create a password.

  2. Install the Uploadcare Blocks library: To use Uploadcare Blocks, you will need to include the Blocks library in your HTML code. You can do this by adding the following script tag to your HTML:

 <script src="https://ucarecdn.com/libs/blocks/2.x/uploadcare.min.js" charset="utf-8"></script>
  1. Add a file input element to your HTML: To create a file uploading form using Uploadcare Blocks, you will need to add a file input element to your HTML code. You can do this by using the following HTML: 
 <lr-file-uploader-regular name="my_file"></lr-file-uploader-regular>

This will create a file input element that will be used to select and upload files.

  1. Initialize the Uploadcare Blocks library: To initialize the libaby set the Uploadcare public key when using the lr-file-uploader-regular element. You can use the --cfg-pubkey attribute as a CSS context property. For example: 
 <style> lr-file-uploader-regular { --cfg-pubkey: "your_public_key"; } </style>

Replace “your_public_key” with your actual Uploadcare public key, which you can find in your account settings. This will set the public key for the file uploader and allow it to connect to the Uploadcare API.

Ha! This time it does not work!

Let’s ask the robot why!

The link you provide, that should install the library, is broken

I apologize for the broken link. The correct URL for the Uploadcare Blocks library is unpkg.com/@uploadcare/blocks@0.14.3/web…. You should use this URL to include the Blocks library in your HTML code.

To include the Blocks library in your HTML, you will need to add the following script tag to your HTML:

<script src="https://unpkg.com/@uploadcare/blocks@0.14.3/web/blocks-browser.min.js" charset="utf-8"></script>

This will include the Blocks library in your HTML, allowing you to use the Blocks library to build file uploaders and file handling workflows.

Well, mister smarty-pants, it looks like you’ve forgot something:

Thanks, now the script works, but it looks like styles are not loaded. Here’s what I see in the console: “CSS Data error: --icon-upload”. How to fix the styles?

To fix the CSS errors that you are seeing in the console, you will need to include the CSS styles for the Uploadcare Blocks library in your HTML.

You can do this by adding the following link element to the head of your HTML:

<link href="https://unpkg.com/@uploadcare/blocks@0.14.3/web/file-uploader-regular.min.css" rel="stylesheet">

This will include the CSS styles for the Blocks library in your HTML, and should fix the CSS errors that you are seeing in the console.

Alternatively, you can use the css-src attribute on the lr-file-uploader-regular element to specify the URL of the CSS styles for the Blocks library. For example:

<lr-file-uploader-regular name="my_file" css-src="https://unpkg.com/@uploadcare/blocks@0.14.3/web/file-uploader-regular.min.css"
></lr-file-uploader-regular>

It works!

We’ve managed to upload a file using the code the bot suggests:

Alright. When I uploaded an image to Uploadcare, how do I inject it to the webpage? I also want to resize it to 256×256px

To display an image that you have uploaded to Uploadcare in your webpage, you will need to use an img element and set the src attribute to the URL of the image on Uploadcare’s servers.

For example:

<img src="https://ucarecdn.com/image_id/">

Replace “image_id” with the actual ID of the image that you want to display. You can find the ID of the image in the response that the Blocks library returns when you upload the image.

To resize the image to a specific size, you can use the -/resize/ operation in the URL of the image. For example, to resize the image to 256×256 pixels, you can use the following URL:

<img src="https://ucarecdn.com/image_id/-/resize/256x256/">

This will display a 256×256 pixel version of the image. You can specify any size that you want by changing the values in the resize operation.

Easy-peasy:

Now, let’s sum up our experiment without italic.

Conclusion

To be honest, when we started the experiment we did not believe that ChatGPT could deal with it. Sure, it’s a cool toy, and it even knows how to generate code examples related to your problem. But we are surprised that the bot knows a lot about our new features, recent documentation, and so on.

It feels like you’re talking to an assistant who can google for you.

After the first rapture had passed, we asked ChatGPT more programming-related questions. We would say that it generates code that looks like it should work. If you do not know how to solve the problem, the code you get can mislead you because it is too convincing.

Besides, ChatGPT makes mistakes. In this article, we’ve encountered only one — the broken link. But the more questions you ask, the more connected the bot’s answers are. Sometimes they become too connected and start to affect each other. E.g. ChatGPT adapts replies to the language or programming paradigm you prefer. This “feature” sometimes leads to broken or unoptimized code.

Anyway, ChatGPT is still a handy tool to use. There are some use cases:

  1. To collect a broad overview of some topics. E.g. you may ask about scientific concepts or discoveries. When the bot answers, you may ask about relations to other concepts, key people in the field, and so on. It’s like reading those Wikipedia snippets in Google, but you can get such a snippet for any of your questions. Try: "Tell me, please, what Alan Turing’s most popular paper was about?"

  2. To handle interactive chats on your website or app (no way!). Or to help a human in a call center to find an answer in a bunch of documentation.

  3. To generate blocks of boring text, like legal documents or official letters. Sure, a lawyer should review the output, but the result is not that bad already. Try: "I’m creating an Android game that does not collect any data. It is not even connected to the Internet. Do you mind generating the privacy policy for the game?"

  4. To extract SEO keywords from the provided text. But we will see what Google thinks about it.

  5. To simplify the description of a complex thing to the desired level. E.g. try to copy-paste a science term definition from Wikipedia and ask ChatGPT to simplify it for a second-grade student.

  6. To solve puzzles together. It can solve crosswords with ease. If you try hard, you can even explain the bot Wordle rules and get help with today’s word!

Finally, if you have a tech company, it’s interesting to see whether the bot can use your service. The result of such a check may trigger you to improve the docs or to clarify the purpose of your product.

Give it a try, and, who knows, you may find ChatGPT a place in your toolset.

P.S. By the way, we generated an OpenGraph image and the image you see below via Midjourney, while ChatGPT helped us to write the text on that images. Gosh, what a time to be alive!

Originally published by Igor Adamenko in Uploadcare Blog

item.title: 如何通过消除 N+1 数据库查询来提高 Rails 应用程序的性能 | How to Improve the Performance of Your Rails App by Eliminating N+1 Database Queries
item.description:
粗糙的帕特尔

发表于 1 月 12 日

我的观察是，当开发人员开始抱怨 Rails 无法扩展时，他们遇到了典型的问题：应用程序在处理少量记录/请求时工作得很好，但现在我们的业务在增长，我们的代码却遇到了麻烦紧跟。

当应用程序从数据库中检索数据然后遍历该数据的结果时，或者当对先前查询的每个结果运行查询时，就会发生这种情况。

如果您的应用程序足够小并且请求和查询很少，那么这很好用。 但是，如果您希望保持应用程序的可伸缩性，则必须从中消除 N+1 问题。

让我们看一个包含两个链接模型的示例：

 # app/models/Company.rb class Company < ApplicationRecord belongs_to :user end # app/models/user.rb class User < ApplicationRecord has_many :companies end

让我们打印所有公司的标题和用户的用户名。

 Company.all.each do |Company| puts "#{Company.title} was written by #{Company.user.username}" end The above code works, but it makes far too many independent database queries: Company Load (0.5ms) SELECT "companies".* FROM "companies" User Load (0.3ms) SELECT "users".* FROM "users" WHERE "users"."id" = ? LIMIT ? [["id", 1], ["LIMIT", 1]] User Load (0.3ms) SELECT "users".* FROM "users" WHERE "users"."id" = ? LIMIT ? [["id", 5], ["LIMIT", 1]] User Load (0.2ms) SELECT "users".* FROM "users" WHERE "users"."id" = ? LIMIT ? [["id", 6], ["LIMIT", 1]] User Load (0.3ms) SELECT "users".* FROM "users" WHERE "users"."id" = ? LIMIT ? [["id", 9], ["LIMIT", 1]] User Load (0.3ms) SELECT "users".* FROM "users" WHERE "users"."id" = ? LIMIT ? [["id", 10], ["LIMIT", 1]] User Load (0.3ms) SELECT "users".* FROM "users" WHERE "users"."id" = ? LIMIT ? [["id", 15], ["LIMIT", 1]]

那是 7 个独立的数据库查询。 在每次迭代中，一个查询用于加载构建，N 个查询用于加载分支，因此得名 N+1 查询。 这种加载方式效率极低。

如果我们有 10000 个用户，我们需要连接到数据库 10001 次。 远程数据库连接不是免费的，每一个都会造成严重的性能损失。

在 Rails 中预加载

与延迟加载相反，当代码一执行查询就加载资源时，就会发生预加载。 作为查询的一部分，它还会加载相关实体。
我们需要减少独立数据库查询的数量，以提高前面示例的性能。

这是在 Rails 中通过预加载关联关系或使用单个查询收集相关数据来实现的。

这在 Rails 中非常容易。 在我们的示例中，我们需要做的就是将 .includes(:user) 附加到我们现有的数据库查询

Company.includes(:user).each do |Company| puts "#{Company.title} was written by #{Company.user.username}" end Company Load (103.7ms) SELECT "companies".* FROM "companies" User Load (32.1ms) SELECT "users".* FROM "users" WHERE "users"."id" IN (?, ?, ?, ?, ?) [["id", 1], ["id", 5], ["id", 6], ["id", 9], ["id", 10], ["id", 15]]

我们现在只用两个查询就得到了相同的结果。 这一次，查询的数量要高得多。 为了将所有数据加载到内存中，我们只使用了两个查询：一个加载公司，另一个加载相关用户。
即使在 10,000 个用户之后，我们的应用程序也只会使用两个数据库查询。

使用 Bullet Gem 消除 N+1 查询

消除 N+1 查询的第一步是确保您的工程团队了解问题并了解可以优化的新查询。

审查拉取请求和分享知识是一个很好的起点，但即使对细节一丝不苟，错误仍然会进入生产代码。 为了解决这个问题，我们一直努力实现自动化，并让 CI 步骤提醒我们潜在的问题。 它监视和报告低效查询。

设置子弹宝石

  • 要在您的 Rails 项目上设置 Bullet，首先将它添加到您的 Gemfile

使用 generate 命令启用 Bullet gem
bundle exec rails g bullet:install

  • 然后，在您的 app/environments/development.rb 中添加以下内容以在开发模式下启用 Bullet：

    •  config.after_initialize do Bullet.enable = true Bullet.bullet_logger = true Bullet.console = true Bullet.alert = true Bullet.airbrake = true end

    通过以上设置，每个 N+1 查询都将记录在 log/bullet.log 日志文件中。 例如，我们的构建和分支示例将产生：

     USE eager loading detected Company => [:user] Add to your finder: :includes => [:user]

    带异常处理的 N+1 预防

    我们可以指示 Bullet 抛出异常，如果代码中不小心引入了 N+1 查询，这将导致生成红色。 我们几乎可以保证没有 N+1 查询会通过引发异常和构建失败来进入生产代码。

    需要在你的文件中添加这个配置
    Bullet.raise: raise errors, useful for making your specs fail unless they have optimized queries

     config.after_initialize do Bullet.enable = true Bullet.bullet_logger = true Bullet.console = true Bullet.alert = true Bullet.raise = true # raise an error if an n+1 query occurs end

    如果您有任何建议或觉得这个博客可以改进，请发表评论。

    谢谢，
    苛刻的 Umaretiya

    一旦暂停，harsh_u115 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    解除暂停后，harsh_u115 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，harsh_u115 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 harsh_u115 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 harsh_u115 的方法：

    降低 harsh_u115 的所有帖子的可见度

    报告其他不当行为

    取消标记 harsh_u115 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Harsh patel

    Posted on Jan 12

    My observation is that when developers start complaining that Rails can't scale, they are running into the typical problem: the application worked just fine with a small number of records/requests, but now that our business is growing, our code is having trouble keeping up.

    N+1 queries are one of the common causes of performance degradation.

    What exactly is the N+1 query problem?

    One of the frequent performance antipatterns in ORMs is the N+1 query problem. It occurs when an application retrieves data from the database and then loops through the results of that data, or when a query is run on each result of the prior query.

    If your application is small enough and there are few requests and queries, this works just fine. However, if you want to keep your application scalable, you must remove the N+1 problem from it.

    Let's look at an example with two linked models:

    # app/models/Company.rb
class Company < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :user
end

# app/models/user.rb
class User < ApplicationRecord
  has_many :companies
end

    Let's print the title and user's username for all the companies.

    Company.all.each do |Company|
  puts "#{Company.title} was written by #{Company.user.username}"
end

The above code works, but it makes far too many independent database queries:

Company Load (0.5ms)  SELECT "companies".* FROM "companies"
  User Load (0.3ms)  SELECT "users".* FROM "users" WHERE "users"."id" = ? LIMIT ?  [["id", 1], ["LIMIT", 1]]
  User Load (0.3ms)  SELECT "users".* FROM "users" WHERE "users"."id" = ? LIMIT ?  [["id", 5], ["LIMIT", 1]]
  User Load (0.2ms)  SELECT "users".* FROM "users" WHERE "users"."id" = ? LIMIT ?  [["id", 6], ["LIMIT", 1]]
  User Load (0.3ms)  SELECT "users".* FROM "users" WHERE "users"."id" = ? LIMIT ?  [["id", 9], ["LIMIT", 1]]
  User Load (0.3ms)  SELECT "users".* FROM "users" WHERE "users"."id" = ? LIMIT ?  [["id", 10], ["LIMIT", 1]]
  User Load (0.3ms)  SELECT "users".* FROM "users" WHERE "users"."id" = ? LIMIT ?  [["id", 15], ["LIMIT", 1]]

    That is 7 separate database queries. In each iteration, one query is used to load the builds, and N queries are used to load the branch, hence the name N+1 queries. This loading method is extremely inefficient.

    We'll need to connect to the database 10001 times if we have 10000 users. Remote database connections are not free, and each one imposes a significant performance penalty.

    Eager Loading in Rails

    In contrast to lazy loading, eager loading occurs when a query loads a resource as soon as the code is executed. As part of the query, it also loads related entities.
    We need to reduce the number of independent database queries in order to improve the performance of the previous example.

    This is accomplished in Rails by eager loading associated relations, or collecting related data with a single query.

    This is very easy in Rails. In our example, all we need to do is attach .includes(:user) to our existing database query

    Company.includes(:user).each do |Company|
  puts "#{Company.title} was written by #{Company.user.username}"
end

Company Load (103.7ms)  SELECT "companies".* FROM "companies"
  User Load (32.1ms)  SELECT "users".* FROM "users" WHERE "users"."id" IN (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)  [["id", 1], ["id", 5], ["id", 6], ["id", 9], ["id", 10], ["id", 15]]

    We now got the same result with only two queries. This time, the number of queries is much higher. To load all of the data into memory, we only used two queries: one to load the companies and another to load the associated users.
    Even after 10,000 users, our app would only use two database queries.

    Eliminating N+1 Queries with the Bullet Gem

    The first step toward eliminating N+1 queries is to ensure that your engineering team understands the problem and is aware of new queries that can be optimised.

    Reviewing pull requests and sharing knowledge is a good place to start, but errors can still find their way into production code even with meticulous attention to detail. To combat this, we always strive to automate and have the CI step alert us to potential problems. Bullet is a fantastic tool for this purpose. It monitors and reports on inefficient queries.

    Setting up Bullet Gem

    • To set up Bullet on your Rails project, first add it to your Gemfile

    gem "bullet" or

    gem install bullet

    • Enable the Bullet gem with generate command
      bundle exec rails g bullet:install

    • Then, add the following in your app/environments/development.rb to enable Bullet in development mode:

    config.after_initialize do
  Bullet.enable = true
  Bullet.bullet_logger = true
  Bullet.console = true
  Bullet.alert = true
  Bullet.airbrake = true
end

    With the above setup, every N+1 query will be logged in the log/bullet.log log file. For example, our example with builds and branches will produce:

    USE eager loading detected
  Company => [:user]
  Add to your finder: :includes => [:user]

    N+1 Prevention with exception handling

    We can instruct Bullet to throw an exception, which will result in red builds if a N+1 query is accidentally introduced into the code. We can almost guarantee that no N+1 query will enter production code by raising an exception and failing your builds.

    Need to add this config in your file
    Bullet.raise: raise errors, useful for making your specs fail unless they have optimized queries

      config.after_initialize do
      Bullet.enable = true
      Bullet.bullet_logger = true
      Bullet.console = true
      Bullet.alert = true
      Bullet.raise = true # raise an error if an n+1 query occurs
    end

    If you have any suggestions or feel that this blog could be improved, please leave a comment.

    Thanks,
    Harsh Umaretiya

    Once suspended, harsh_u115 will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, harsh_u115 will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by harsh_u115 will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If harsh_u115 is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Note:

    Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag harsh_u115:

    Make all posts by harsh_u115 less visible

    harsh_u115 consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging harsh_u115 will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 提高工作效率的 10 大 chrome 扩展 | Top 10 chrome extensions for better productivity
item.description:
1. 您是否正在寻找在使用 Google Chrome 时提高工作效率和保持井井有条的方法？ 不要再观望！ 在这篇博文中，我们将探索 10 大提高工作效率的 Chrome 扩展程序。 从任务管理到语法检查器，这些扩展将帮助您掌控工作并充分利用时间。 无论您是忙碌的专业人士还是试图保持专注的学生，这些扩展肯定适合每个人。 因此，让我们开始吧，看看 10 大提高工作效率的 Chrome 扩展程序。

提高工作效率的 10 大 chrome 扩展：

该扩展允许用户自定义在一天中的特定时间屏蔽哪些网站或网站类别（例如社交媒体或娱乐）。 这对于那些发现自己在应该工作的时候却在分散注意力的网站上浪费时间的人很有帮助。 用户还可以为工作需要访问的特定网站设置例外。 总的来说，StayFocused 对于那些在使用互联网时难以保持专注并避免分心的人来说是一个有用的工具。

该扩展程序允许用户设置阻止他们访问分散注意力的网站的时间。 如果用户在此期间尝试访问被阻止的网站，则用户种植的虚拟树将会死亡。 这可以切实提醒用户分心的后果，并鼓励用户保持专注。 除了核心功能外，Forest 还提供“团队”形式的社交组件，用户可以在其中竞争，看看谁种的虚拟树最多。 扩展产生的部分产品也捐赠给种植真树和致力于打击森林砍伐的组织。 总体而言，Forest 是一种独特而有效的工具，可帮助用户在使用互联网时保持专注，从而提高工作效率。

该扩展允许用户创建任务、设置截止日期并跟踪他们的进度。 此外，除了其核心功能，Todoist 还提供了一系列功能来帮助用户有效地管理他们的任务，包括子任务、优先级以及将任务分类到项目中的能力。 总的来说，Todoist 是一个强大的工具，适合任何希望改善任务管理并保持井井有条的人。

该扩展程序提供实时语法和拼写检查，并针对语法和拼写检查等各种写作问题提出更正建议，并针对语法、标点符号、单词选择和句子结构等各种写作问题提出更正建议。 它对专业人士、学生和任何想提高写作技巧的人特别有用。 用户还可以使用建议的更正作为学习工具来扩展他们的语法规则知识并改进他们的写作风格。 此外，Grammarly 提供了一个浏览器插件，可以与 Gmail、LinkedIn 和 Slack 等其他应用程序无缝集成，从而可以在不同平台上轻松应用其建议。 总的来说，Grammarly 是一个综合性的写作工具，可以帮助用户提高写作技巧，写出优美、专业的文章。

该扩展允许单击一次保存文章、图像和其他网络内容，还可以突出显示文本、添加注释和剪辑整个页面或页面的特定部分。 使用 Pocket，您可以轻松地保存来自 Web 浏览器、社交媒体帐户或任何其他来源的内容，并在任何设备上访问它。
将内容保存到 Pocket 后，它会存储在一个列表中，您可以随时访问和浏览。 此工具适用于 Google Chrome、Mozilla Firefox、Safari 和 Microsoft Edge，可用于各种流行的在线零售商。 通过在用户购物时在后台运行，该扩展程序会在结账时自动应用任何相关的优惠券或折扣，帮助用户在网上购物时省钱。 此外，该扩展还提供价格比较功能，并可与各种奖励计划相结合。 这样可以做出更明智的购买决定，并最终节省网上购物的费用。

该扩展适用于 Google Chrome，可将打开的选项卡转换为可以保存并稍后恢复的列表格式。 这允许用户整理他们的浏览器，提高性能并轻松访问选项卡。 该扩展包括按主题或网站对选项卡进行分组以及在组内搜索选项卡的功能。 此外。 此扩展可以通过减少打开的选项卡数量以及允许用户轻松访问和组织他们打开的选项卡来帮助提高工作效率。

势头是一个角色； 仪表板包括带有时间和天气的问候语、励志名言、待办事项列表以及设置当天重点的功能。 该扩展还包括一个时间跟踪功能，允许用户监控他们的网站使用情况并相应地进行调整。 总之，前 10 个 Chrome 生产力扩展可以帮助您在使用 Google Chrome 时保持专注、有条理和积极性。 从阻止分散注意力的网站到管理您的密码，这些扩展程序提供了多种工具来帮助您充分利用时间。 无论您是忙碌的专业人士还是试图保持专注的学生，这些扩展都可以帮助您在工作中保持领先地位并实现您的目标。 因此，如果您希望提高工作效率并保持井井有条，请务必尝试这些扩展！



原文:
  1. Are you looking for ways to increase productivity and stay organised while using Google Chrome? Look no further! In this blog post, we will be exploring the top 10 Chrome extensions for productivity. From task management to grammar checkers, these extensions will help you stay on top of your work and make the most of your time. Whether you're a busy professional or a student trying to stay focused, these extensions sure have something for everyone. So let's get started and take a look at the top 10 Chrome extensions for productivity.

Top 10 chrome extensions for better productivity:

StayFocused is a productivity extension for Google Chrome that helps users stay focused on their work by blocking distracting websites. The extension allows users to customize which websites or categories of websites (such as social media or entertainment) are blocked during certain times of the day. This can be helpful for people who find themselves wasting time on distracting websites when they should be working. StayFocused can be configured to block websites for specific periods of time (such as during working hours), or for a certain number of minutes per day. Users can also set exceptions for specific websites that they need to access for work. Overall, StayFocused is a useful tool for anyone who struggles to stay focused and avoid distractions while using the internet.

Forest is a Google Chrome extension designed to improve productivity by providing a visual representation of the user’s focus. The extension allows users to set times during which they are prevented from accessing distracting websites. If a user attempts to access a blocked website during this time, a virtual tree planted by the user will die. This serves as a tangible reminder of the consequences of succumbing to distractions and encourages users to maintain their focus. In addition to its core functionality, Forest also offers a social component in the form of “teams” where users can compete to see who can plant the most virtual trees. A portion of the products generated by the extension is also donated to organizations that plant real trees and work to combat deforestation. Overall, Forest is a unique and effective tool for helping users increase the productivity by staying focused while using the internet.

Todoist is a task management extension for Google Chrome that helps users organize their work and improve their productivity. The extension allows users to create tasks, set deadlines, and track their progress. Todoist also supports collaboration, allowing users to assign tasks to team members and set up alerts to remind them of upcoming deadlines. In addition, to its core functionality, Todoist offers a range of features to help users manage their tasks effectively including subtasks, priorities, and the ability to categorize tasks into projects. Todoist is also available on other platforms, making it easy for users to access and update their to-do-list from anywhere. Overall, Todoist is a powerful tool for anyone looking to improve their task management and stay organized.

Grammarly is a writing enhancement extension for Google Chrome designs to assist users in producing error-free writing. The extension provides real-time grammar and spelling checks and suggests corrections for various writing issues such as grammar and spelling checks and suggests corrections for various writing issues such as grammar, punctuation, word choice, and sentence structure. It is particularly useful for professionals, students and anyone who aims to improve their writing skills. Grammarly offers advanced checks for academic, business and specific genres of writing such as technical or creative. Users can also use the suggested corrections as a learning tool to expand their knowledge of grammar rules and improve their writing styles. Additionally, Grammarly, offers a browser add-on that is seamlessly integrated with other applications such as Gmail, LinkedIn, and Slack, making it easy to apply its suggestions while working on different platforms. Overall, Grammarly is a comprehensive writing tool that can help users improve their writing skills and produce polished, professional writing.

Evernote Web Clipper is a browser extension that allows users to save and organize web content directly into their Evernote account. The extension allows for the saving of articles, images, and other web content with a single click, as well as the ability to highlight text, add notes, and clip entire pages or specific parts of a page. Evernote Web Clipper is available on Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, and Safari browsers, enabling users to access saved content from any device and share it with others.

Pocket is a browser extension and a standalone app that allows you to save web content (such as articles, videos and images) for later viewing. With Pocket, you can easily save content from your web browser, social media accounts, or any other source, and access it on any device.
Once you have saved content to Pocket, it is stored in a list that you can access and browse through at any time. Pocket also has a feature called “Recommendations” that suggests articles and other content based on your saved items and your reading history. Pocket is available as a chrome extension for Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, and Safari, as well as a standalone app for mobile devices and tablets.

LastPass is a password management tool and browser extension that allows users to securely store and manage their passwords and sensitive information. The LastPass extension is available for Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, and other popular web browsers, and enables users to create strong, unique passwords, automatically fill in login credentials, and access their account from any device using a master password. LastPass also offers security and privacy features such as two-factor authentication password sharing, and emergency access, providing added protection for user accounts and sensitive information.

Honey is a browser extension that enables users to automatically find and apply coupon codes during online checkout. This tool is available for Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Safari and Microsoft Edge, and can be utilized on a wide range of popular online retailers. By running in the background while users shop, the extension automatically applies any relevant coupons or discounts at checkout, helping users save money on their online purchases. In addition, the extension offers a price comparison feature and can integrate with various rewards programs. This allows for more informed buying decisions and ultimately savings on online shopping.

OneTab is a browser extension that facilitates the management and organization of open tabs within a web browser. The extension, available for Google Chrome, converts open tabs into a list format that can be saved and later restored. This allows users to declutter their browser, enhance performance and easily access the tabs. The extension includes a feature to group tabs by topic or website, and also to search tabs within a group. Additionally. OneTab provides an option to easily export and import the lists of tabs to different computers or browsers. This extension can help with productivity by reducing the number of open tabs, and by allowing users to easily access and organize the tabs they do have open.

Momentum is a persona; dashboard extension for Google Chrome that provides users with a personalized, customizable page when a new tab is opened. The dashboard includes a greeting with the time and weather, an inspirational quote, a to-do list, and the ability to set a focus for the day. The extension also includes a time tracking feature which allows users to monitor their website usage and adjust accordingly. Momentum helps users stay organized and motivated throughout their day.

In conclusion, the top 10 Chrome extensions for productivity can help you stay focused, organized, and motivated while using Google Chrome. From blocking distracting websites to managing your passwords, these extensions offer a variety of tools to help you make the most of your time. Whether you're a busy professional or a student trying to stay focused, these extensions can help you stay on top of your work and achieve your goals. So if you're looking to increase your productivity and stay organized, be sure to give these extensions a try!

item.description:
在本文中，我们将看到如何在 Laravel 9 中创建用于 XSS 保护的中间件。

跨站点脚本是一种安全漏洞，可以在某些 Web 应用程序中找到。 在laravel 9中我们使用中间件来防止对网站安全的XSS攻击。 这是非常必要的保护，以防止网站上的 XSS 攻击或任何其他网络攻击。

那么，让我们看看laravel 9 create middleware for XSS protection，laravel 9 create middleware and XSS protection in laravel 9。

通过 XSS 过滤器，我们可以从我们的输入值中删除 HTML 标记，而且为了安全起见，删除 HTML 标记也非常重要。

输入清理是一种安全协议，用于检查、过滤和清理来自应用程序用户的数据输入。

反射型 XSS，恶意脚本来自当前的 HTTP 请求。

  • 存储型 XSS，恶意脚本来自网站的数据库。
  • 基于 DOM 的 XSS，漏洞存在于客户端代码中，而不是服务器端代码中。

    In this article, we will see how to create middleware for XSS protection in laravel 9.

    Cross-site scripting is a type of security vulnerability that can be found in some web applications. XSS attacks enable attackers to inject client-side scripts into web pages viewed by other users.

    In laravel 9 we use middleware to prevent XSS attacks on website security. It's very necessary protection from XSS attacks or any other cyberattack on the website.

    So, let's see laravel 9 create middleware for XSS protection, laravel 9 creates middleware and XSS protection in laravel 9.

    The XSS filter through we can remove the HTML tag from our input value and also it's very important to remove the HTML tag for security.

    Input sanitization is a security protocol for checking, filtering, and cleaning data inputs from app users.

    Types of XSS attacks?
    There are three main types of XSS attacks:

    • Reflected XSS, where the malicious script comes from the current HTTP request.
    • Stored XSS, where the malicious script comes from the website's database.
    • DOM-based XSS, where the vulnerability exists in client-side code rather than server-side code.

item.title: 物联网创新促进了自动驾驶巴士的使用 | IoT Innovations Boost Use of Self-Driving Buses
item.description:
无线串口

发表于 1 月 12 日

自动驾驶公交车为智慧城市带来了一种环保的交通方式。 这些车辆配备人工智能和物联网技术，已准备好改变地铁交通。 由于零尾气排放，电动汽车对环境更有利。 城市公交车虽然比汽车好，但仍然是温室气体排放的主要来源。 作为公共交通计划的一部分，世界各地的城市都在推广电动汽车，以减少碳足迹。 在许多情况下，这些电动汽车是自动驾驶的，并依靠物联网技术发挥作用。

物联网支持

一个例子来自 EasyMile 和 7StarLake。 两家公司开发了 EZ10 自动驾驶巴士，该巴士在全球 30 个国家/地区的 200 个地点运营，其中包括美国的 16 个地点。 这款 12 座车辆包括一个内置的自动轮椅坡道。 它们的最高时速为 15 英里/小时，可以在雨、雪和雾的混合驾驶条件下运行。 这辆 13 英尺的自动驾驶汽车结合了雷达、激光雷达、GPS、里程计和人工智能，可以在预定的路线上行驶。 这些自动驾驶公交车配备了人工智能摄像头，可以360度观察公交车周围的情况。 计算机视觉识别标牌并检测车辆前方或周围的物体； 公共汽车和公共汽车站上的内置 2D 和 3D 摄像头有助于确定乘客是否在等候。

核心计算

他们分析传感器数据以确定定位、导航、能量和速度，并与中央交通枢纽进行通信。 该系统使用人工智能和计算机视觉来识别驾驶线路，确定何时加速或减速，并监控安全系统。 此外，集成传感器允许 EZ10 避开障碍物。 实时数据处理使自动驾驶公交车能够与其他车辆互动，并在必要时超车或让路。 人工智能和物联网技术可实现车对车或车对基础设施的通信，因此市政当局可以建设智能走廊并为其居民提供移动即服务。

车队的管理

每辆 EZ10 自动驾驶公交车都配备了 EasyMile 的车队管理系统 EZFleet，它将数据传递到监管中心。 该系统实时监控每辆车的位置、分配的路线和目的地，以及预计到达时间。 它可以根据流量动态调整流量。 乘客通过应用程序请求服务，EZFleet 决定最适合搭载他们的班车和路线。 管理系统还可以重新安排行程以搭载更多乘客，或随着乘客数量的增加派遣更多车辆，同时保持服务水平。 这是个好消息，因为到 2028 年，自动驾驶汽车市场预计将达到 40 亿美元。

自动驾驶巴士不仅更环保，而且通常也更安全。 自动驾驶汽车不会被噪音或智能手机分心，也不会感到疲劳。 未来的智慧城市将包括自动公交车，使公共交通更清洁、更环保、更安全。

原文:

wirelessserial

Posted on Jan 12

Self-driving buses bring an environmentally friendly transportation option to smart cities. Equipped with artificial intelligence and IoT technologies, these vehicles are ready to transform subway transportation. With zero tailpipe emissions, electric vehicles are better for the environment. The EPA claims that EVs emit less greenhouse gas emissions than gasoline-powered vehicles over their entire life cycle, even after taking into account manufacturing, charging, and driving. City buses, while better than cars, are still a major source of greenhouse gas emissions. Cities around the world are rolling out electric vehicles as part of their public transport plans in an effort to curb their carbon footprint. In many cases, these EVs are self-driving and rely on IoT technology to function.

Internet of things support

An example comes from EasyMile and 7StarLake. The two companies developed the EZ10 autonomous bus, which operates at 200 locations in 30 countries around the world, including 16 U.S. locations. This 12-seat vehicle includes a built-in automatic wheelchair ramp. They have a top speed of 15 mph and can operate in mixed driving conditions of rain, snow and fog. The 13-foot self-driving car combines radar, lidar, GPS, odometry and artificial intelligence to drive on a predetermined route. These autonomous buses are equipped with artificial intelligence cameras that can observe the situation around the bus in 360 degrees. Computer vision recognizes signage and detects objects in front of or around the vehicle; built-in 2D and 3D cameras — on buses and bus stops — help determine if passengers are waiting.

Core computing

At the heart of the EZ10 autonomous bus are 7StarLake's CPT320 computers, powered by Intel® Core™ processors, which process data for all aspects of autonomous driving. They analyze sensor data to determine positioning, navigation, energy and speed, and communicate with central transportation hubs. 7StarLake's SR10A computer with Intel® Core™ processor supports in-bus entertainment. The system uses artificial intelligence and computer vision to recognize driving lines, determine when to speed up or slow down, and monitor safety systems. Additionally, integrated sensors allow the EZ10 to avoid obstacles. Real-time data processing enables self-driving buses to interact with other vehicles and overtake or give way if necessary. AI and IoT technologies enable vehicle-to-vehicle or vehicle-to-infrastructure communications, so municipalities can build smart corridors and provide mobility-as-a-service to their residents.

Fleet management

Each EZ10 self-driving bus is equipped with EZFleet, EasyMile's fleet management system, which passes data to the supervision center. The system monitors each vehicle's location, assigned route and destination, and estimated time of arrival in real time. It can dynamically adjust traffic according to traffic flow. EZFleet also supports on-demand travel. Passengers request service through an app, and EZFleet decides which shuttle and route are best suited to carry them. The management system can also reroute travel to pick up more passengers, or dispatch additional vehicles as passenger numbers increase, while maintaining service levels. EasyMile's software operates independently of the vehicle, enabling other device manufacturers to integrate into their self-driving vehicles. That's great news, as the autonomous vehicle market is expected to reach $4 billion by 2028.

Self-driving buses are not only greener, but generally safer as well. Self-driving cars won't be distracted by noise or smartphones, and won't get tired. Future smart cities will include autonomous buses to make public transport cleaner, greener and safer.

Hans-Peter Grahsl - Jul 18 '21

item.title: 1519. 具有相同标签的子树中的节点数 | 1519. Number of Nodes in the Sub-Tree With the Same Label
item.description:
问题陈述：

给定一棵树（即没有环路的连通无向图），它由 n 个节点组成，编号从 0 到 n - 1，正好有 n - 1 条边。 树的根是节点 0，树的每个节点都有一个标签，标签是字符串标签中给出的小写字符（即编号为 i 的节点的标签为 labels[i]）。

返回一个大小为 n 的数组，其中 ans[i] 是第 i 个节点的子树中与节点 i 具有相同标签的节点数。

树 T 的子树是由 T 中的一个节点及其所有后代节点组成的树。

示例 1：
输入： n = 7，edges = [[0,1],[0,2],[1,4],[1,5],[2,3],[2,6]], labels = "abaedcd “
输出： [2,1,1,1,1,1,1]
解释：节点 0 的标签为“a”，它的子树也有标签为“a”的节点 2，因此答案为 2。请注意，任何节点都是其子树的一部分。
节点 1 具有标签“b”。 节点 1 的子树包含节点 1,4 和 5，因为节点 4 和 5 的标签与节点 1 不同，答案只是 1（节点本身）。

示例 2：
输入： n = 4，edges = [[0,1],[1,2],[0,3]], labels = "bbbb"
输出： [4,2,1,1]
解释：节点2的子树只包含节点2，所以答案为1。节点3的子树只包含节点3，所以答案为1。节点1的子树包含节点1和2 ，两者都有标签“b”，因此答案是 2。

节点 0 的子树包含节点 0、1、2 和 3，都带有标签 'b'，因此答案是 4。

示例 3：
输入： n = 5, edges = [[0,1],[0,2],[1,3],[0,4]], labels = "aabab"
输出： [3,2,1,1,1]

约束：

  • 边[i].length == 2
  • 解决方案：
    代码：

     class Solution { private int[] ans; private List<List<Integer>> adjlist; private Set<Integer> visited; public int[] countSubTrees(int n, int[][] edges, String labels) { ans= new int[n]; adjlist=new ArrayList<>(n); for(int i=0;i<n;i++){ adjlist.add(new ArrayList<>()); } for(int[] e: edges){ adjlist.get(e[0]).add(e[1]); adjlist.get(e[1]).add(e[0]); } visited=new HashSet<Integer>(n); dfs(0,labels); return ans; } private int[] dfs(int node, String labels){ visited.add(node); int[] count=new int[26]; for(int adj:adjlist.get(node)){ if(!visited.contains(adj)){ int[] adjcount=dfs(adj,labels); for(int i=0;i<26;i++){ count[i]+=adjcount[i]; } } } char ch=labels.charAt(node); count[ch-'a']++; ans[node]=count[ch-'a']; return count; } }

    一旦暂停，harshrajpal 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

    一旦解除暂停，harshrajpal 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，harshrajpal 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 harshrajpal 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 harshrajpal：

    降低 harshrajpal 的所有帖子的可见度

    报告其他不当行为

    取消标记 harshrajpal 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Problem Statement:

You are given a tree (i.e. a connected, undirected graph that has no cycles) consisting of n nodes numbered from 0 to n - 1 and exactly n - 1 edges. The root of the tree is the node 0, and each node of the tree has a label which is a lower-case character given in the string labels (i.e. The node with the number i has the label labels[i]).

The edges array is given on the form edges[i] = [ai, bi], which means there is an edge between nodes ai and bi in the tree.

Return an array of size n where ans[i] is the number of nodes in the subtree of the ith node which have the same label as node i.

A subtree of a tree T is the tree consisting of a node in T and all of its descendant nodes.

Example 1:
Input: n = 7, edges = [[0,1],[0,2],[1,4],[1,5],[2,3],[2,6]], labels = "abaedcd"
Output: [2,1,1,1,1,1,1]
Explanation: Node 0 has label 'a' and its sub-tree has node 2 with label 'a' as well, thus the answer is 2. Notice that any node is part of its sub-tree.
Node 1 has a label 'b'. The sub-tree of node 1 contains nodes 1,4 and 5, as nodes 4 and 5 have different labels than node 1, the answer is just 1 (the node itself).

Example 2:
Input: n = 4, edges = [[0,1],[1,2],[0,3]], labels = "bbbb"
Output: [4,2,1,1]
Explanation: The sub-tree of node 2 contains only node 2, so the answer is 1. The sub-tree of node 3 contains only node 3, so the answer is 1. The sub-tree of node 1 contains nodes 1 and 2, both have label 'b', thus the answer is 2.

The sub-tree of node 0 contains nodes 0, 1, 2 and 3, all with label 'b', thus the answer is 4.

Example 3:
Input: n = 5, edges = [[0,1],[0,2],[1,3],[0,4]], labels = "aabab"
Output: [3,2,1,1,1]

Constraints:

  • 1 <= n <= 105
  • edges.length == n - 1
  • edges[i].length == 2
  • 0 <= ai, bi < n
  • ai != bi
  • labels.length == n
  • labels is consisting of only of lowercase English letters.

Solution:
Code:

class Solution {
    private int[] ans;
    private List<List<Integer>> adjlist;
    private Set<Integer> visited;
    public int[] countSubTrees(int n, int[][] edges, String labels) {
        ans= new int[n];
        adjlist=new ArrayList<>(n);
        for(int i=0;i<n;i++){
            adjlist.add(new ArrayList<>());
        }
        for(int[] e: edges){
            adjlist.get(e[0]).add(e[1]);
            adjlist.get(e[1]).add(e[0]);
        }
        visited=new HashSet<Integer>(n);
        dfs(0,labels);
        return ans;

    }
    private int[] dfs(int node, String labels){
        visited.add(node);
        int[] count=new int[26];
        for(int adj:adjlist.get(node)){
            if(!visited.contains(adj)){
                int[] adjcount=dfs(adj,labels);
                for(int i=0;i<26;i++){
                    count[i]+=adjcount[i];
                }
            }
        }
        char ch=labels.charAt(node);
        count[ch-'a']++;
        ans[node]=count[ch-'a'];
        return count;

    }
}

Spas Poptchev - Nov 11 '22

ZeeshanAli-0704 - Nov 9 '22

ZeeshanAli-0704 - Oct 19 '22
